Full-time placements make paid work alongside the course difficult. The NHS Learning Support Fund grant is also available on eligible pre-registration MSc programmes in England.

supportingReasons: Three-year full-time undergraduate degree approved by the NMC, with clinical placements throughout.
concerns: Most BSc Midwifery courses require GCSE 4/C in maths, English and a science. You will typically need to gain these before applying.
BSc Midwifery requires a Level 3 qualification such as A-levels, a relevant T Level, or an Access to Higher Education Diploma (Nursing/Midwifery/Health).

Full-time placements (including nights and weekends) make paid work alongside the course difficult. The NHS Learning Support Fund provides a non-repayable grant of at least £5,000 per academic year for eligible students in England.

immediateActions.title: Look into how to gain the required GCSEs
immediateActions.description: Contact a local further education college about GCSE resit options in maths, English and a science, or a Functional Skills Level 2 route. Ask each shortlisted midwifery provider which they accept.

immediateActions.title: Explore Access to Higher Education Diplomas in Nursing/Midwifery
immediateActions.description: Look up Access to HE Diplomas (Nursing, Midwifery or Health) at local FE colleges. These are widely accepted in place of A-levels for midwifery programmes.

immediateActions.title: Check NHS Learning Support Fund eligibility
immediateActions.description: Read the NHS Business Services Authority page on the Learning Support Fund and check whether your circumstances would qualify you for the training grant, parental support and specialist subject payment.

supportingReasons: Postgraduate pre-registration programme for graduates in a relevant health or science subject, typically two years full time.
concerns: The MSc pre-registration route is designed for graduates who are not already registered nurses; the shortened conversion route is typically faster in your case.

considerations: As an NMC-registered adult nurse, the shortened conversion route is likely to be the fastest path onto the midwives part of the register.
